lines.line: # Challenges and Controversies in Job Offer

> The subject delves into the complications they faced after being offered a position at the State Department of Education. They highlight issues related to racial politics, salary negotiations, and the resulting tension and misunderstandings that arose during the hiring process.
** challenges, job offer, controversies, racial politics, salary negotiation, tensions **

lines.line: # Impact and Insights at the Urban League

> The interviewee reflects on their time at the Urban League, describing the organization’s role and their contributions. They discuss identifying and challenging the systemic issues hindering the effectiveness of the Urban League in the black community and their efforts to push for organizational change.

story.title: Femi Bogle Assagai Oral History Part 4
story.description: The interview discusses a professional struggle involving a woman who, upon arriving in a new country, applied for a job as the administrator for the Apple program within the State Department of Education. Despite her qualifications, she faced significant challenges, including a controversial hiring decision influenced by racial politics and salary disputes, ultimately leading her to decline the position. She eventually worked for the Urban League of Greater Hartford as education director, contributing to various initiatives alongside Esther Bush, and had insights into financial and organizational dynamics within the nonprofit sector. The conversation also touches on her later involvement in civil rights work.
